name: Amara Diallo
profile_completeness_notes: 10th grade. No SAT/ACT taken yet. No essays written. Only 2 years of transcript. AP exams not yet taken. Profile is strong in foundation but completely undifferentiated — evaluations should be heavily forward-looking with specific guidance on differentiation.

ap_courses.note: Sophomore year. On track for a 4.

academic.note: ETHS offers a strong honors and AP track. Amara has taken every honors course available in her lane. Her grades are consistent and strong. She is on track to take 4-5 APs junior year and 5+ senior year.

test_scores.note: No standardized tests taken. Plans to prepare seriously for the SAT starting summer before junior year. Family has identified Khan Academy and a community-funded test prep program at her school.

activities.name: Student Government
activities.description: Elected by classmates to represent the sophomore class. Led initiative to add more diverse authors to the 10th-grade English curriculum. Organized a successful school supply drive for freshman orientation.

activities.name: Girls Who Code Club
activities.description: Co-founded chapter sophomore year with a teacher sponsor. 22 members in first year. Organized two "intro to coding" workshops for 8th graders at a nearby middle school. Amara has no prior coding background — she started this to build the skill while creating community.

activities.name: JV Soccer
activities.description: Two-year JV player. Solid contributor. Not at varsity level but committed team member. Played club soccer in middle school.

activities.name: French Club
activities.description: Participates in cultural events and conversation practice. Native-level French strengthens her involvement — often serves as a conversation partner for less advanced students.

activities.name: Evanston Public Library — Youth Volunteer
activities.description: Assists in the children's section: reading programs, shelf organization, helping kids find books. Began volunteering to fulfill NHS service hours but continued because she enjoys it.

intended_career: Entrepreneurship or nonprofit leadership, with interest in economic development in West African communities

essays.main_essay_theme: Potential topic: growing up between two cultures — her parents' Guinean household and the predominantly white North Shore suburb — and what it means to translate between worlds. Strong raw material but topic is common; execution will determine quality.
essays.supplemental_notes: No essays written. Writing ability appears solid based on English grades but is untested in the personal essay format. She will need to develop a specific, distinctive voice rather than relying on general themes of identity or perseverance.
essays.writing_style: Unknown at this stage. Teachers describe her as a clear, organized writer with good instincts. Essay coaching recommended before applications.

hooks: Black / West African — URM consideration at many schools
Geographic: Evanston is competitive but not overrepresented
additional_info: Parents immigrated from Guinea in 2001. Father works in hotel management; mother is a registered nurse. Family income approximately $72,000/year with three children. Will need need-based financial aid but is not at maximum Pell Grant threshold. First person in immediate family to pursue a 4-year US college. Extended family in Guinea and France have university-educated members, so family broadly understands higher education value.
special_circumstances: Profile at this stage is genuinely solid but generic. Strong GPA, decent activities, first-gen, URM — these are all positive signals, but the application tells no distinctive story yet. The most important work between now and applications is developing a spike or a narrative. Guidance counselor notes she has 'enormous potential but needs to find her thing.' The Girls Who Code co-founding is the most promising seed of differentiation.

high_school.name: Evanston Township High School
high_school.committee_context: Large, diverse, high-performing public school in one of the most educated suburbs of Chicago. ETHS is well known to admissions officers at selective colleges — it sends students to elite institutions regularly and is not considered a grade-inflating school. A 3.88 UW here is legitimately strong. The school has a 31% free/reduced lunch population and significant demographic diversity, which counselors note in school profile letters.
high_school.gpa_adjustment_note: 3.88 UW is read at face value — strong performance at a reputable, well-regarded public school with no significant grade inflation.
